operation: First, a workplane aligned to the front reference plane is initialized. A spline is drawn passing through three coordinate points: (0, 0), (3, 5), and (6, 0). A straight line segment is added from the end of the spline back to the origin (0, 0) to form a closed 2D profile. This closed profile is then extruded 4 units in the direction normal to the front workplane to produce the final 3D solid.
geometry: This is a uniform cross-section prismatic solid. Its cross-section perpendicular to the extrusion axis is a closed curved triangular shape, with two straight edges meeting at a sharp vertex and one convex curved edge connecting the free ends of the straight edges. The two end faces of the prism are identical copies of this curved triangular profile, joined by two flat rectangular side faces and one continuous curved side face.
function: This curved wedge-shaped component is designed for use as a spacer, aerodynamic fairing insert, or custom structural fitting. It features a smooth curved edge to avoid sharp contact points or improve flow performance, with consistent thickness for easy installation into matching cutouts or recesses.
